verifier_values.criteria: States that the patient has resistant hypertension.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
From "Cards Pt 10 script for Jan 10.pdf": This patient is already taking a combination of a thiazide and an ACE inhibitor, as well as a beta blocker. She continues to have elevated blood pressure. At today's visit, her blood pressure remains elevated. Therefore, she has resistant hypertension.

"Resistant hypertension is defined as BP above goal despite treatment with 3 antihypertensive medications with complementary mechanisms of action, including a diuretic at maximally tolerated doses or BP at goal but requiring ≥4 medications (Figure 8)."
https://www.ahajournals.org/doi/pdf/10.1161/CIR.0000000000001356

verifier_values.criteria: States that screening for primary aldosteronism in adults with resistant hypertension.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:

verifier_values.criteria: States that primary aldosteronism is the single most likely diagnosis to be ruled out.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
The patient has a history of hypertension, and their blood pressure was found to be elevated at today's clinic visit compared to the most recent one. They are currently taking three antihypertensive medications and have resistant hypertension. Laboratory results show a normal potassium level. The assessment utilizes the 2025 American College of Cardiology/American Heart Association joint committee clinical practice guidelines on hypertension. It is recommended to screen for primary aldosteronism in adults with resistant hypertension, regardless of potassium status, as primary aldosteronism is the most likely diagnosis to be ruled out. Furthermore, primary aldosteronism is listed at an incidence of at 25%, much higher than other potential causes such as renal artery stenosis (5%) and pheochromocytoma (0.6%).

https://www.ahajournals.org/doi/pdf/10.1161/CIR.0000000000001356

verifier_values.criteria: States that the single most important laboratory test to rule out primary aldosteronism is a plasma aldosterone-to-renin ratio.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
"The combined interpretation of the plasma aldosterone concentration, renin activity, and aldosterone to renin activity ratio is currently the most accurate and reliable means of screening for primary aldosteronism."

https://www.ahajournals.org/doi/pdf/10.1161/CIR.0000000000001356
